repositories.brief_summary: Ephemeron GC-cycle orphaning fix and a cross-compilation `BINDIR` fix landed, while new PRs proposed instance-variable lifting and `Dynarray` unsafe accessors.
repositories.new_features_summary: Ephemeron GC-cycle correctness fix, cross-compilation `BINDIR`/Windows build fixes, and a `promote-menhir` bootstrap fix landed on trunk.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed a **race between GC phase transitions and ephemeron orphaning**: a domain could start a next-cycle stop-the-world section just as another domain terminated and orphaned its ephemerons, leaving them unprocessed across cycles; the fix adds a barrier that re-checks the phase-complete condition before all domains commit to moving on ([Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), review by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez), [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [5427daa](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/commit/5427daaf60b4a0ce83c9657dff2bf3433cb30eb1), [ad86f17](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/commit/ad86f17bf8f2d5210d9d64404a666b5dc1ec459f))
- Fixed **`Makefile.cross`** to override `BINDIR` while building cross-compilers, so bytecode programs built during the process pick up the host `ocamlrun` rather than a missing target one, fixing an OCaml/Solo5 cross-compiler build reported against 5.5.0 RC1 ([@shym](https://github.com/shym), [#14869](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14869), [c5f30f1](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/commit/c5f30f146682043d1267ecd0ae26f131b8941d51))
- Landed a batch of **Windows cross-compilation build fixes**: preventing clang-cl from synthesizing an unresolved `wcslen` call in `header.obj` via `-fno-builtin-wcslen`, moving `tmpheader` cflags/ldflags/libs detection into `configure`, quoting the `CC` variable passed to `FLEXLINK_BUILD_ENV` so flags containing spaces survive, factoring out C-compiler selection for building FlexDLL, dropping the unsupported 32-bit Cygwin target, and trimming unnecessary `installcross` placeholder files ([Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), [#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850))
- Fixed the **`promote-menhir` Makefile target**, which copies Menhir's standard library into `boot/menhir` for bootstrapping: it relied on `menhir --suggest-menhirLib`, whose output format changed upstream so `menhirLib.mli` is no longer installed; switched to the more robust `menhir --dump-menhirLib` ([Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [#14862](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14862))
- Reworked the **expect-test runner** (`testsuite/tools/expect.ml`) to run the default, `-principal`, and (soon) `-rectypes` test variants together in a single invocation instead of separate passes, adding `reset_raise_count` in `lambda.mli` and `reset_weak_names` in `out_type.mli` so compiler-internal counters can be reset between variants without a fresh process ([Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), part of [#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643))
repositories.activity_summary: Instance-variable lifting, `Dynarray.unsafe_get`/`unsafe_set`, and `Sys.mkdir` permission docs were opened, while domain-stack-size, Haiku, and memory-model-doc PRs continued review.
repositories.activity:
- [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) opened a PR **lifting instance-variable loads** to the start of the enclosing method when the variable is immutable, fixing the case reported in [#14861](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14861) where a `Gc.finalise_last` callback registered in a class `initializer` implicitly captures `self` and prevents collection ([#14868](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14868))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened **`Dynarray.unsafe_get`/`unsafe_set`**, benchmarks showing `unsafe_get` about 2.5x faster and `unsafe_set` about 1.4x faster than the safe versions, followed by an extended documentation discussion with [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) on how to word the safety caveats around concurrent access and module boundaries ([#14864](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14864))
- [Martin Constantino–Bodin](https://github.com/Mbodin) opened a **documentation fix for `Sys.mkdir` permissions**, clarifying that the mode argument is interpreted bit-by-bit rather than digit-by-digit as with shell `chmod`, refined after feedback from [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to reuse the `Unix.file_perm` doc-comment style ([#14863](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14863))
- [@shym](https://github.com/shym) opened and merged the `Makefile.cross` **`BINDIR`** fix, cherry-picked into the 5.5.0 release on [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s recommendation given it only touches cross-compilation and had been approved by [Sébastien Hinderer](https://github.com/shindere) ([#14869](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14869))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened and merged the **`promote-menhir`** target fix, with naming and changelog suggestions from [Sébastien Hinderer](https://github.com/shindere) and confirmation from [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) on the upstream Menhir change that motivated it ([#14862](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14862))
- [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) continued iterating on the **configurable domain stack size** PR (`OCAMLRUNPARAM=S=`), moving the platform-specific code into `caml_plat_thread_create` in `runtime/caml/platform.h` and adding Windows thread-attribute destruction after review from [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860), related to [#14195](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14195))
- The **ephemeron GC-cycle fix** ([#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813)) and the associated **weak-pointer/ephemeron testsuite repro cases** for [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349) ([#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) both merged, closing out a multi-week investigation into randomized-testing failures found by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)'s multicoretests harness
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) kept refining the **memory-model documentation** PR for `memory.c`, clarifying that fences described are emitted by the OCaml compiler (not the C compiler) and tightening the new `[MMPS]` "publication safety" note based on [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s comprehension questions ([#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857))
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) continued the **`-rectypes` expect-test** PR; [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) benchmarked the change over 500 runs and found the testsuite runtime impact negligible (112.06s vs 111.97s average) ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643))
- [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) continued the **Haiku port** PR covering Unix-socket test adjustments, single-threaded `setlocale`, and missing `SIGIO` support, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) flagging that matching on invalid-argument error text is too brittle and suggesting the test instead detect and skip unsupported signals ([#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14773))
- [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez)'s draft **new GC-pacing logic** PR had a reported test failure traced to a rounding problem that forced the GC into many very small slices, now fixed ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796))
- [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) opened PRs adding **RISC-V compressed-instruction support** (an 8.7-11% code-size reduction on `ocamlopt.opt` by enabling the ISA's "C" extension and re-tuning `Lswitch` codegen and register allocation) and a **bounds check in `caml_read_section_descriptors`** to guard against overflow when loading bytecode with an unreasonable number of section descriptors ([#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873), [#14872](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14872))
repositories.discussions_summary: A discussion questioned why the typedtree now wraps plain types in an empty `TPoly` after upgrading to 5.5.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) asked whether it's expected for the typedtree to represent a plain `string` type as `TPoly ([], string_typ)` after migrating from 5.3 to 5.5, having hit test failures in code that pattern-matches on the typedtree directly ([#14870](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14870))
repositories.trends_summary: Cross-compilation and Windows build reliability, plus GC/ephemeron correctness, were the two clearest threads of sustained work this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Cross-compilation and Windows build reliability** continued to draw sustained attention: the `Makefile.cross` `BINDIR` fix ([#14869](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14869)), the broader Windows cross-compilation batch ([#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850)), and the ongoing Haiku port ([#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14773)) all landed or progressed in the same window
- **GC and memory-model correctness** work converged this week: the ephemeron orphaning fix ([#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813)) and its regression tests ([#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) merged, the GC-pacing rewrite fixed a rounding bug ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)), and memory-model documentation continued to be refined ([#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857)), alongside a separate discussion on raising the default minor-heap size ([#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)) and a report of `Gc.compact` leaking address space when `vm.max_map_count` is exceeded ([#14865](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14865))
- **`unsafe_*` accessors and their documentation** are getting closer scrutiny: the `Dynarray.unsafe_get`/`unsafe_set` PR ([#14864](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14864)) sparked a detailed back-and-forth on how to phrase concurrency and module-boundary caveats, mirroring past discussion on `Array.unsafe_get`/`unsafe_set`
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributors this week spanned ephemeron/GC correctness, cross-compilation fixes, stdlib documentation, and bug reports on object finalizers, bytecode segfaults, and Windows opam builds.

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion continued on last week's resolver and switch-creation bug reports while two new issues were opened on sandbox reinit and filename typing.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #6938 adding .install root/rootexec sections remained queued while several carried-over issue threads advanced, with #6962 closed after further diagnosis.
repositories.activity:
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) continued [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) adding `root` and `rootexec` sections to `.install` files (would close [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919)), still queued behind [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) kept [#6960](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6960) queued, fixing the openSUSE depexts CI job by forcing dependency resolution to work around a conflict between the `ocaml` and `ncurses-devel` system packages
- [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) and [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) worked through [#6962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6962), a suspected `opam upgrade` resolver regression that broke installing `pa_ppx.8.05.01` with tests on switches below OCaml 5.3; the thread traced the conflict to unreleased dependency bound changes on `ppx_deriving_yojson`, and the issue was closed after relaxing the bound, with [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) noting the package had in fact been released and passed CI via [ocaml/opam-repository#29931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29931)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6968](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6968), reporting that the opam root upgrade/reinit mechanism does not propagate a previously-disabled sandbox setting
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6967) proposing to replace legacy `basename`-typed paths in `OpamFile` with the newer `unix_filename` type for paths that should not be resolved
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) acknowledged [#6965](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6965), confirming that `opam switch create --debug` should surface added repositories when no solution is found, following [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok)'s report of a solver conflict tied to a relocatable repository
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) responded to [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s [#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963) report of `opam` failing on `git ls-files` when the repository owner differs from the running user, flagging it for discussion at a future dev meeting and noting some internal git commands are not run verbosely
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964) request to allow demoting `opam lint` errors to warnings remained open, motivated by opam-repository's wish to reduce Error 46 to a warning (see [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998))
repositories.discussions_summary: The #6962 resolver-bug thread showed that opam packages can gain new dependency bounds without a version bump, complicating install debugging.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#6962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6962), [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) explained to [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) that opam-repository packages can have their dependency bounds updated without a new package version, which can make a previously-installable package stop resolving; the exchange led to identifying an incompatible `ppx_sexp_conv`/`ppxlib` bound combination via [ocaml/opam-repository#29931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29931)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) flagged [#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963) ([Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s `git ls-files` ownership failure) for discussion at a future dev meeting, with a TODO to review why some internal git invocations aren't run verbosely for easier debugging
repositories.trends_summary: Issue activity this week centered on solver diagnosability and typed-path cleanup rather than new feature work.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Multiple threads this week concern making opam's solver and switch-creation failures easier to diagnose: `--debug` not showing repository selection ([#6965](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6965)), unclear guidance after a resolver conflict ([#6962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6962)), and an internal `git ls-files` failure surfacing as an opaque error ([#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963))
- Incremental typed-API cleanup continues with [#6967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6967) proposing `unix_filename` in place of `basename` for unresolved paths in `OpamFile`, alongside the ongoing `.install` file work in [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)
- Coordination between opam and opam-repository policy surfaced twice, in the lint severity request [#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964) (tied to [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998)) and the dependency bound discussion in [#6962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6962) (tied to [ocaml/opam-repository#29931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29931))
repositories.issues_summary: Issue #6967 asks for a straightforward type swap from basename to unix_filename in OpamFile and is tagged easy first issue.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#6967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6967) — **Use `unix_filename` type instead of `basename`**: recently-added `unix_filename` is meant for paths that shouldn't be resolved, but legacy code in `OpamFile` still uses `basename` for the same purpose; opened by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) and labeled `easy first issue` / `AREA: API`

repositories.brief_summary: Merged compatibility fix removing the Stdune.Pid dependency to track an upcoming Dune API change ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
repositories.new_features_summary: Process ids switched from Stdune.Pid to plain int across the server to match Dune's API change ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
repositories.new_features:
- Replaced `Stdune.Pid.t` with plain `int` for process identifiers throughout `diagnostics.ml`/`.mli`, `dune.ml`, `merlin_config.ml`, `ocamlformat.ml`, `ocamlformat_rpc.ml`, and the vendored `lev_fiber.ml`, matching a corresponding API change in [ocaml/dune@39d8a28](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/commit/39d8a28) so ocaml-lsp keeps building against upcoming Dune releases ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
- `Merlin_config.Process`, `Ocamlformat.run_command`, and `Ocamlformat_rpc.Process` no longer wrap/unwrap pids via `Pid.of_int`/`Pid.to_int` before calling `Lev_fiber.waitpid` or `Unix.kill`, simplifying process spawning and termination paths used when running merlin, ocamlformat, and the ocamlformat-rpc subprocess ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
- `Lev_fiber.Process_watcher` now keys its active-process table on `Int` instead of `Pid`, removing the last internal use of the `Pid` module ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
- Updated `CHANGES.md` to document the fix under the "Fixes" section for the next release ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
repositories.activity_summary: The week's only human PR was the merged Stdune.Pid removal ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)), alongside the routine automated flake.lock update PR.
repositories.activity:
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) opened and merged **Avoid Stdune.Pid for process ids** ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)), touching 9 files to keep ocaml-lsp compatible with an upstream signature change in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune).
- The automated **flake.lock: Update** PR ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1437)) remained open, picking up newer `merlin5_3` and `nixpkgs` inputs via the update-flake-lock GitHub Action; it still requires a maintainer to re-trigger CI since Actions do not run automatically on bot-opened PRs.
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on [#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608) weighed dropping the `Pid` module entirely versus a smaller int-based compromise.
repositories.notable_discussions: - On [#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) asked whether ocaml-lsp could **stop using the `Pid` module altogether** rather than just adapting to the new signature, prompting [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) to change the representation to a plain `int` as a compromise between full removal and minimal change.
repositories.emerging_trends: - Continued **API-compatibility tracking** with upstream Dune, following the `Pid`-to-`int` change to preempt breakage from Dune's own refactor ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608), referencing [ocaml/dune@39d8a28](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/commit/39d8a28)).
repositories.contributors_summary: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) authored and merged the week's fix, with review input from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
repositories.contributors:
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) implemented and merged the `Stdune.Pid` removal spanning diagnostics, dune, merlin config, ocamlformat, and the vendored `lev_fiber` process watcher, and updated `CHANGES.md` accordingly ([#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608)).
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) reviewed [#1608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1608), questioning whether the `Pid` module should be dropped entirely and prompting the switch to a plain `int` representation.

repositories.brief_summary: Indexer memory-usage work continued with a new LRU-cache PR from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), alongside a truncated-error-message report from [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
repositories.activity_summary: New LRU-cache indexing PR opened by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) supersedes [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s earlier attempt, under active review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w).
repositories.activity:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) opened [#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079), adding an **LRU cache** to bound indexing memory usage by storing small values alongside their parent, making indexer filenames relative to the working directory, and disabling related-uids compression; the PR builds on the earlier [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050) and is under active review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), who raised points on `On_disk` schema handling, LRU promotion of parents, `relativize` path handling, and de-duplicating `merge + union` logic into `Union_find` ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079)).
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s original **indexer LRU cache** proposal ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050)), which replaced the unbounded index-links `Hashtable` with a bounded LRU, was noted by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) as superseded by the newer [#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079).
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) commented on the long-standing **stack overflow on hover** report for mutually recursive types ([#1755](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1755)), linking it to a possibly related earlier bug ([#822](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/822)).
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on truncated Emacs error messages debated whether the fix belongs in Merlin's server or in editor clients like Eglot.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reported that Emacs, via merlin.el, truncates a multi-line functor-mismatch **error message** to only its last line ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)); [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) explained that the omitted text is delivered as a diagnostic "footnote" that OCaml LSP passes through but that Emacs's built-in Eglot (as opposed to ocaml-eglot) currently discards, meaning the fix may need to land in **merlin clients** (merlin.el, ocaml-eglot, and possibly OCaml.nvim/vim) rather than the server ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)).
- In the same thread, [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) asked whether Merlin has a **testsuite** for error-message formatting so upstream compiler message changes from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) could be caught earlier, and speculated that ongoing compiler work on structured (sexp/json) diagnostic output (ocaml/ocaml#9979, ocaml/RFCs#45, ocaml/ocaml#13766) could eliminate this category of bug entirely ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)).
repositories.trends_summary: Indexer memory usage remains an active area, now in its second review iteration via LRU caching.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Indexer memory optimization** continues as an iterative effort, moving from [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s initial LRU implementation ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050)) to [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)'s more extensive follow-up with detailed line-by-line review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079)).
- Bug triage is increasingly drawing a line between **Merlin-server** and **editor-client** responsibility, as seen in the discussion of where truncated diagnostic text should be fixed across merlin.el, Eglot, and ocaml-eglot ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)).
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue is open regarding truncated error messages in Emacs.
repositories.good_first_issues: - Truncated multi-line **error message** shown by Merlin in Emacs for functor type-mismatch errors, likely requiring a client-side fix in merlin.el or related editor integrations ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)).
repositories.contributors_summary: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), and [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) worked on indexer memory usage while [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw), and [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) engaged on bug reports.
repositories.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) opened the LRU-cache indexing PR ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079)) building on prior work, and responded to review feedback from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) as well as noting that [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050) is superseded by his new PR.
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) provided detailed code review on [#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079), covering `On_disk` schema defaults, LRU parent promotion, path relativization edge cases, de-duplication of `merge + union` logic, and cache-consistency questions around `Serialized`/`Serialized_reused`.
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) authored the original indexer LRU cache proposal ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050)) that motivated the follow-up work in [#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2079).
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reported the truncated error message issue ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)) and raised the question of testsuite coverage for compiler error-message formatting.
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) contributed context on how different editor clients (Eglot, ocaml-eglot) handle diagnostic footnotes in the discussion of [#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080).
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) commented on the older stack-overflow-on-hover issue ([#1755](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1755)), cross-referencing a related report ([#822](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/822)).

repositories.brief_summary: Action runners and sandboxed action execution were stabilized ahead of the 3.24.0 release, alongside env_vars and @rocqproject aliases.
repositories.new_features_summary: Opt-in action runners with sandboxed (bwrap/Landlock) execution, env_vars alias, @rocqproject alias, and default fuzzy patch matching.
repositories.new_features:
- **Action runners** were reintroduced as an opt-in feature via `--action-runner`, along with `--sandbox-actions` to run eligible process-spawning actions inside a bubblewrap-sandboxed worker, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240)), followed by a stabilization series covering RPC startup ordering, process-group handling, and worker lifecycle ([#15244](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15244), [#15247](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15247), [#15249](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15249), [#15252](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15252), [#15254](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15254), [#15256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15256), [#15260](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15260), [#15263](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15263), [#15268](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15268)). Using either flag requires the dune 3.24 release.
- A **Landlock sandboxing backend** for `--sandbox-actions` is under review, adding a `--sandbox-actions-backend` option with `auto`, `bwrap`, and `landlock` choices ([#15231](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15231)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), building on this week's action-runner work.
- `env_vars` was added as an underscore-spelled alias for the `env-vars` field in `env` stanzas ([#15187](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15187)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), resolving the dashes-vs-underscores inconsistency reported in [#11424](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11424); the new spelling requires dune 3.24.
- A new `@rocqproject` alias generates all `_RocqProject` files for a workspace via `dune build @rocqproject` ([#15221](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15221)) by [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai).
- **Fuzzy offset matching** was enabled by default for patch application, matching opam's behavior and easing builds of packages like OxCaml ([#15146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15146), fixes [#15145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15145)) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) — this is a default behavior change affecting all `(patch ...)` actions, not guarded by a lang-version bump.
- Path-valued pforms (`%{bin:...}`, `%{dep:...}`, `%{path:...}`) now expand same-directory paths with a leading `./`, so shell actions like `(bash ...)`/`(system ...)` execute them directly instead of doing a `PATH` lookup ([#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), fixing a regression ([#15147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15147)) from a prior release; the fix was backported directly onto the `3.24.0-rc` branch ([#15229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15229)) after discussion about how disruptive it could be.
- Dune now cleans up build processes it spawned on Linux before starting a fresh build, addressing escaped/orphaned subreaper children left behind on shutdown ([#15239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15239), [#15271](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15271), [#15277](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15277)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **Cram test** output sanitization now correctly rewrites absolute paths containing spaces, including lines with multiple paths ([#15017](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15017), fixes [#14943](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14943)) by [Yahya Ehsan](https://github.com/yussypu).
- RPC connect/bind now handles long Unix socket paths correctly by operating from their containing directory instead of failing on path-length limits ([#15234](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15234), [#15235](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15235), [#15236](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15236)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- A **performance improvement** in the memo layer removed hot-path closures via new `Fiber.Var` combinators ([#15220](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15220)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- `dune init`/`dune start` inside a Makefile-based project now skips files that already exist instead of erroring ([#15218](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15218)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
repositories.activity_summary: Action-runner stabilization dominated merged work while melange internals, cram-test fixes, and 3.24.0-rc backports continued.
repositories.activity:
- Several PRs opened this week remain under review: an RPC cleanup removing the background RPC wrapper ([#15270](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15270)) and a cram-test fix to show partial output on timeout ([#15269](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15269)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), a fix so `dune utop` accepts absolute paths to workspace directories ([#15272](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15272)) by [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7), a CI update to track the latest opam commit for the mirage workflow ([#15273](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15273)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), the Landlock **sandbox-actions** backend ([#15231](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15231)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), a sites-plugin test fix ([#15224](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15224)) by [Petter A. Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal), and an extended test for the bin-pform-from-undeclared-package case ([#15222](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15222)) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan).
- The **action-runner** rework dominated merged activity, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) landing a long chain of refactors and fixes to RPC server lifecycle, process-group handling, and worker startup ordering ([#15227](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15227), [#15232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15232), [#15233](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15233), [#15240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15240), [#15241](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15241), [#15242](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15242), [#15244](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15244), [#15246](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15246), [#15247](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15247), [#15248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15248), [#15249](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15249), [#15250](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15250), [#15251](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15251), [#15252](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15252), [#15254](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15254), [#15255](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15255), [#15256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15256), [#15260](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15260), [#15263](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15263), [#15266](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15266), [#15267](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15267), [#15268](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15268)), plus supporting `stdune` helpers for **process trees**, `Pid.kill`, `Pid.me`, and `Fd.dup` ([#15225](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15225), [#15226](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15226), [#15238](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15238), [#15245](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15245), [#15264](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15264)).
- **Melange** build-mode internals and tests were reworked by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), moving `effective_lib_modes` into `Lib_info` ([#15243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15243)), adding `Compilation_mode.Set` ([#15262](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15262)), and covering merlin-config and `.cmj`-dependency behavior for mixed melange/OCaml libraries ([#15257](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15257), [#15261](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15261), [#15259](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15259)); separately, [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) has an open PR filtering inter-library dependencies per module to cut unnecessary recompilation ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14492)).
- **Cram-test infrastructure** saw several fixes and refactors beyond the path-sanitization fix: unsetting `DUNE_RPC`/`DUNE_BUILD_DIR` in nested `runtest` invocations ([#15237](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15237)) and reducing binding scope in the cram runner ([#15265](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15265)).
- **RPC/socket handling** was reworked further, moving server lifecycle management into the RPC library and creating the lifecycle server eagerly ([#15217](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15217), [#15233](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15233)).
- The **3.24.0 release** moved into its release-candidate phase, tracked in [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620) and [#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14621), with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) preparing the release and backporting the path-pform fix onto the `3.24.0-rc` branch ([#15229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15229)).
- Other in-progress feature work includes **shell completion** for `dune build`/`dune init` commands via cmdliner 2.1.1, tested across bash/zsh/powershell ([#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14779)) by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS), cross-compilation install-file generation of `root`/`rootexec` sections pending an upstream opam change ([#14600](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14600)) by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian), and directory renaming support for `(include_subdirs qualified)` ([#13691](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13691)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) also added a repository skill routing review requests through a checklist ([#15275](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15275)), an internal contributor-tooling change.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion covered a ratchetting cram-test proposal and how disruptive the path-pform ./-prefix fix should be.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) proposed "ratchetting cram tests" — a cram-test variant that only allows promotion when a tracked violation count (e.g. `Obj.magic` occurrences) decreases ([#15230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15230)); [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) pointed out the same effect can already be achieved with a `(rule (alias runtest) (corrections produce) ...)` action today.
- Reviewers debated how disruptive the `./`-prefix fix for path pforms should be: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) initially proposed guarding it behind a `dune lang` version bump, while [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) discussed exactly what it would break before it shipped unguarded as a direct fix for the [#15147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15147) regression ([#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156)).
- The 3.24.0 release tracker ([#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)) recorded a real-world regression report against Jane Street's `bonsai` package caused by `bin`-pform path handling, which directly informed the fix landed in [#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156).
- The long-standing dashes-vs-underscores naming inconsistency around `env-vars` was resolved by adding the `env_vars` alias, closing [#11424](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11424) via [#15187](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15187).
repositories.trends_summary: Sandboxing infrastructure and release engineering for 3.24.0 drove most of the week's development focus.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Process-sandboxing infrastructure** (bubblewrap now, a Landlock backend in progress) is being actively hardened ahead of release, dominating this week's merged PR volume through the action-runner stabilization chain ([#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240), [#15231](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15231)).
- **Release engineering** is ramping up for 3.24.0, with fixes backported from `main` onto the `3.24.0-rc` branch ([#15229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15229)) and the release tracker ([#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)) actively triaging regressions from real-world packages.
- Several internal API additions (`Fd.dup`, `Pid.kill`, `Pid.me`, Linux process-tree helpers, `Fiber.Var` combinators) reflect ongoing consolidation of process/fiber-management primitives in `stdune`/`fiber` that support the action-runner work ([#15220](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15220), [#15225](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15225), [#15226](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15226), [#15238](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15238), [#15264](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15264)).
repositories.issues_summary: Three open good-first-issues cover named targets, SIGTERM buffer flushing, and dune init .gitignore generation.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- Support **named targets** so build outputs can be referenced by a stable name rather than path ([#3309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3309)).
- Flush output buffers when a process receives `SIGTERM` ([#3882](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3882)).
- Have `dune init proj` create a `.gitignore` containing `_build` for new projects ([#9567](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9567)).
repositories.contributors_summary: rgrinberg led action-runner/RPC work while Alizter, anmonteiro, Sudha247, and others contributed features, tests, and reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) led the action-runner/sandboxing rework, merging the bulk of related refactors and fixes ([#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240) and the stabilization series in the activity above), reworked RPC server lifecycle and long-socket-path handling ([#15217](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15217), [#15233](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15233), [#15234](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15234)-[#15236](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15236)), landed the `env_vars` alias ([#15187](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15187)), a memo-layer performance fix ([#15220](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15220)), the `dune init` fix for Makefile-based projects ([#15218](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15218)), opened the in-progress Landlock sandbox backend ([#15231](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15231)), reviewed the cram path-sanitization fix ([#15017](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15017)), and discussed the path-pform breaking-change tradeoffs on [#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156).

repositories.brief_summary: [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) opened a draft fix for the driver's link-path handling, closed the same day without merging ([#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449)).
repositories.activity_summary: A draft PR addressing incorrect link paths in the odoc driver was opened and closed without merging ([#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449)).
repositories.activity:
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) opened a draft PR to fix the odoc **driver**'s handling of link paths ([#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449)), noting that the driver added every library of a package to each compilation unit's `-L` link path instead of only the unit's own declared dependencies; for packages with sibling libraries that share module names but do not depend on each other (the example given is `eliom.server` and `eliom.client`), a module defined in both — a functor result like `Content.Html` — could resolve to the wrong sibling, so the client API documentation ended up showing the server's `Content.Html`
- The proposed fix in [#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449) builds the link path from `lib_deps` (the unit's own library plus its declared dependencies), mirroring how the existing `-I` include paths are already constructed, and was verified by hand against the Eliom packages where client and server libraries share module names
- [#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449) was marked as a draft and closed the same day it was opened without merging, with GitHub reporting the branch as conflicting with the base, leaving the underlying link-path issue unresolved
repositories.contributors_summary: [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) contributed a draft fix for a driver link-path bug affecting documentation of sibling libraries with shared module names.
repositories.contributors: - [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) diagnosed and proposed a fix for a bug in odoc's driver where compilation units were linked against every library in a package rather than only their declared dependencies, causing modules with the same name in sibling libraries (such as `eliom.server` and `eliom.client`) to resolve incorrectly in generated documentation; the change was submitted as a draft PR ([#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1449)) and verified by hand against the Eliom packages, though it was closed the same day without merging due to a conflicting branch state

repositories.brief_summary: ocaml.org published release pages for OCaml 4.14.4 and 5.5.0 alongside routine automated Planet blog updates.
repositories.new_features_summary: New ocaml.org release pages for OCaml 4.14.4 and OCaml 5.5.0, each with full changelogs and install instructions.
repositories.new_features:
- Published the **OCaml 5.5.0 release page** with installation instructions and a changelog covering module-dependent functions, polymorphic function parameters, external types, generalized local definitions, garbage collector improvements, and around 60 new standard library functions (including `String.split_first`, `String.replace_first`, `String.includes`, `String.find_first_index`), authored by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- Published the **OCaml 4.14.4 release page** documenting backported bug fixes, including two CVE fixes (CVE-2026-34353 for a size-overflow check in `caml_ba_reshape`, and CVE-2026-41083 for `Filename.quote_command` argument quoting on Windows), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- Updated the OCaml 4.14.3 and 5.4.1 release pages to unset their `is_lts`/`is_latest` flags now that 4.14.4 and 5.5.0 are current ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684), [#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- Automated **Planet blog aggregation** added new posts, including Ocsigen's "what we're working on" retrospective, robur.coop's piece on Grafana for MirageOS unikernels, the 16 Jun 2026 OCaml Weekly News digest, and posts from janestreet, tunbury and anil ([#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))
repositories.activity_summary: Review and merge of the 4.14.4 and 5.5.0 release pages dominated the week alongside routine Planet scraping and a documentation-gap report.
repositories.activity:
- The **OCaml 5.5.0 release page** merge involved extensive review, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggesting numerous manual wording and code-example corrections and [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) merging once commit f06d84d7c addressed the requested changes ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- The **OCaml 4.14.4 release page**, covering safe bug fixes cherry-picked from the 5.x branch, was reviewed by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) before merging ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- Routine **Planet feed scraping** continued to surface new community blog posts while repeatedly flagging the same unreachable or misconfigured feeds for the mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver and patricoferris blogs ([#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))
- [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) opened an issue noting the two most recent `patricia-tree` opam package releases (0.14.0 and 0.13.0) have no documentation on ocaml.org, possibly related to earlier documentation-generation issues ([#3437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3437), [#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451)) ([#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on the 5.5.0 release PR touched on manual-update process and a branch-protection quirk blocking merges.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On the **OCaml 5.5.0 release PR**, [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) asked about the process for updating the online manual and noted a related PR on `ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals` had been stalled for 8 months ([ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals#12](https://github.com/ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals/issues/12)); he also observed that ocaml.org appeared to block merging while a change request was outstanding, which [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) said was not intended behavior ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) raised missing documentation for the latest `patricia-tree` releases, linking it to two previously reported documentation-generation issues ([#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685), [#3437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3437), [#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451))
repositories.trends_summary: Two compiler release pages landed in the same week, and automated Planet scraping keeps surfacing the same broken feeds unresolved.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Two compiler releases, the patch release **4.14.4** and the feature release **5.5.0**, had their ocaml.org pages published in the same week, a heavier-than-usual release documentation load ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684), [#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- Automated `[scrape]` PRs from `github-actions` continued at a near-daily cadence, consistently reporting the same set of unreachable or misconfigured Planet feeds (mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver, patricoferris) without resolution ([#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))
repositories.contributors_summary: Florian Angeletti authored both release pages; Anil Madhavapeddy and sabine reviewed them; Cuihtlauac Alvarado merged the 5.5.0 page; Dorian Lesbre filed a documentation gap report.
repositories.contributors:
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) authored the release pages for both **OCaml 4.14.4** ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684)) and **OCaml 5.5.0** ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)), and raised process questions about manual updates during review of the latter
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reviewed the **OCaml 5.5.0 release page**, suggesting numerous wording and code-example corrections, and commented on the branch-protection behavior encountered during merging ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) merged the **OCaml 5.5.0 release page** after confirming the requested review changes had been addressed ([#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) reviewed the **OCaml 4.14.4 release page**, suggesting a metadata correction ([#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) opened an issue reporting missing documentation for recent `patricia-tree` package releases ([#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685))

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 and 4.14.4 compiler releases landed alongside Windows CI hardening and numerous package releases.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 official release, an OCaml 4.14.4 security bugfix release, and Windows CI/depext fixes landed this week.
repositories.new_features:
- Official **OCaml 5.5.0** compiler release added, including `ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, `ocaml-variants`, `ocaml-system` and `ocaml-src` packages, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075)), followed by a fix restoring the **relocatable compiler build** template that had been accidentally reverted ([#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085))
- **OCaml 4.14.4 bugfix release** containing two standard-library security fixes and an ARM64 compilation fix, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054)), with the `avoid-version` flag removed once validated ([#30067](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30067))
- `conf-pkg-config` fixed for **OpenBSD** to use the base-system `pkg-config` instead of the retired `pkgconf` port, by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082), closing [#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080))
- Windows CI reworked to **reduce depext-driven switch recompilations** without breaking lockfiles, and the temporary `conf-pkg-config` workaround removed in favour of a new `conf-allegro5.2` package built on `conf-pkg-config.5`, by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058))
- Windows CI workflow bumped `actions/checkout` from v6 to v7 and switched the compiler selector from `ocaml-base-compiler,conf-pkg-config` to `"5"` (dependabot, [#30076](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30076))
- New package **ktdeque**, a purely-functional double-ended queue with worst-case O(1) operations extracted from a machine-checked Rocq proof, added by [Yann Régis Gianas](https://github.com/yurug) ([#30060](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30060))
- `omod` 0.0.5 gains **OCaml 5.5.0 support** and restored `ocamlnat` compatibility, by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) ([#30063](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30063))
- `baby` re-released to fix incompatibility with **OCaml 5.5** in prior releases, by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) ([#30084](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30084), reported in [#30083](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30083))

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions covered a dune 3.24.0 regression, package-utility policy for example/name-reservation packages, and OpenBSD pkg-config handling.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **dune 3.24.0** release thread surfaced test-suite regressions in cppo, camomile and lablgtk3 tied to a breaking change in `%{bin:...}` variable handling, flagged by @olafhering, with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) confirming the change is expected to affect only invalid usage and planning follow-up bounds ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092))
- Maintainers reaffirmed the repository's **utility-for-users policy** for package submissions, questioning the standalone **agentxm-example-tinyflags** package by [Craig Smitham](https://github.com/craigsmitham) ([#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) and rejecting the **ioqwerty** name-reservation package by [Anton Matiash](https://github.com/amatiash) ([#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049))
- [Sebastien Marie](https://github.com/semarie) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) clarified that `pkg-config` has shipped in the **OpenBSD** base system since release 7.8, resolving the `conf-pkg-config` special-casing question ([#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080), fixed by [#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082))
- [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported a **bad checksum** on the `wol` package family, hosted on tangled.org rather than GitHub, tagging [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) and [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) ([#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089))
repositories.trends_summary: OCaml 5.5 ecosystem migration, ongoing Windows CI hardening, and new AI-agent-tooling package submissions were the week's clearest trends.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem migration**: the official 5.5.0 compiler release ([#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075)), a relocatable-build fix ([#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085)), and compatibility updates in `baby` ([#30084](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30084)) and `omod` ([#30063](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30063)) all landed within the same week
- Continued **Windows CI hardening** across linked PRs reducing depext-driven switch recompilation and modernizing the workflow ([#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058), [#30076](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30076))
- **AI/agent-tooling packages** entering the repository — a Programmable Agent Runtime SDK ([#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)) and an AgentXM example package ([#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) — prompting maintainers to reaffirm the utility-for-users submission bar
- **Automated batch releases** from upstream CI bots continue to make up a large share of merged activity (ocurrent [#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069), ocaml-ffmpeg [#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), liquidsoap [#30057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30057), landmarks [#30071](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30071), ocaml-version [#30077](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30077)/[#30056](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30056))
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributors released compiler versions, packages, and CI fixes, while maintainers reviewed new-package submissions against repository policy.

repositories.brief_summary: js_of_ocaml 6.4.0 was released with OCaml 5.5 support, OxCaml support, WASI 0.1, and a pure-Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b runtime.
repositories.new_features_summary: 6.4.0 ships OCaml 5.5/OxCaml support, WASI 0.1, Stack-Switching effects, Promise/Fetch APIs, and a pure-Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b runtime.
repositories.new_features:
- js_of_ocaml **6.4.0** was released this week, with **OCaml 5.5.0 support** as the headline driver, closing out the release-tracking issue ([#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241), [#2197](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2197), [#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220))
- **OxCaml support** added to the compiler ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105), [#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225))
- Compiler gains a **variable coalescing pass** plus faster, byte-identical variable-naming, free-variable and shape-computation passes ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166), [#2321](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2321), [#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198))
- New `--build-config`/`--apply-build-config` **compiler flags** capture and replay build configuration, and the compiler now puts more values into **global variables** ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177), [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211))
- **Cosmetic minification** in compact mode — `!0`/`!1` booleans, trimmed `0.x` fractions, normalised exponents (`1e+05` → `1e5`), and backtick strings when they reduce escapes — while `--pretty` output is unchanged ([#1117](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1117))
- Wasm backend adds **dynlink and toplevel** support, so the OCaml toplevel now runs on the Wasm backend ([#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186))
- **WASI 0.1 support** lets wasm_of_ocaml output run on standalone WASI runtimes such as wasmtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))
- wasm_of_ocaml gains an alternative **effects implementation** based on the Stack Switching proposal, and the Wasm runtime ships pure-Wasm **zstd and BLAKE2b** implementations so unmarshalling compressed values and computing digests no longer depend on JavaScript shims — and both now work under WASI, which has no JavaScript ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189), [#2249](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2249))
- Wasm runtime implements the legacy `num` library's **`nat` primitives** (previously no-op stubs), so arbitrary-precision integers and rationals now work on the Wasm backend as they do on JavaScript ([#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263))
- Runtime adds initial **quickjs-ng** support ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229))
- New **`Promise`** module — type-safe bindings to JavaScript promises, even for `'a Promise.t Promise.t` — with Lwt interop (`Js_of_ocaml_lwt.Promise.to_lwt`/`of_lwt`) and Promise-typed `Dom_html` bindings for `requestFullscreen`, `requestPointerLock`, `exitFullscreen`, `mediaElement.play`, `imageElement.decode` and `Animation.{finished,ready}`; note that `mediaElement.play` now returns `unit Promise.t` instead of `unit` as a **breaking change** ([#2031](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2031))
- New **`Fetch`** and **`Abort`** modules bind the Fetch API with a typed `AbortController`/`AbortSignal` for request cancellation ([#596](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/596))
- Additional `Dom_html` bindings, a new **`Performance`** module, and `Console` bindings for `table`, `count`, `countReset` and `timeLog` ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221), [#2248](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2248), [#2350](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2350))
- `Dom_svg` aligned with **SVG 2** — new `graphicsElement`/`geometryElement` parents, writable coordinates, the full `SVGFE*` filter-primitive family, and every SVG element is now an event target; **breaking**: drops the SVG 1.1-only `getTransformToElement` ([#519](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/519))
- Popover API bindings and `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` added to the library ([#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734), [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070))
- Dead legacy-browser DOM code removed (IE `attachEvent`/`createElement`/`cancelBubble` fallbacks, prefixed `requestAnimationFrame`, Firefox 3.x `File.fileName`); **breaking**: drops the obsolete Gecko `MouseScrollEvent`/`_DOMMouseScroll` bindings ([#2350](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2350))
- Documentation now records **which opam package provides each module**, closing a long-standing request ([#2366](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2366), [#607](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/607))
- Manual and doc-link cleanup: factual corrections across the manual, corrected `//If: !flag` syntax, repaired broken odoc cross-references, and fixed stale published-documentation URLs, plus deriving_json/Lwt edge-case fixes and a JS-toplevel fallback in the Lwt toplevel example ([#2362](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2362), [#2365](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2365))
- ocsigen.org hosted docs gain cross-links to TyXML, ReactiveData and Lwt, and the manual now lists the full Ocsigen family in its hosted-package table; `doc/wodoc` updated for wodoc's new defaults ([#2322](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2322), [#2323](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2323), [#2351](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2351), [#2364](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2364), [#2363](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2363))
- Supported-engine baseline documented as ES2020 / Node.js 18+ / QuickJS-NG, replacing the stale "Node.js 16 / ECMAScript 6" claim in the README ([9c64b5c](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/9c64b5c97193df21f2333823db0fa99c20e5b198))
repositories.activity_summary: Release 6.4.0 shipped while runtime-review cleanups, a Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b rewrite, and test-infra consolidation continued.
repositories.activity:
- Three PRs remain open going into next week: [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) proposed ordering the manual sidebar with `@children_order` for ocaml.org packaging ([#2359](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2359)), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened prep work for **OxCaml 5.4** ([#2342](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2342)), and [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) proposed switching the manual's JavaScript examples to conventional `//Provides:`-style comments ([#2320](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2320))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) drove the **6.4.0 release** to completion — consolidating and compacting the `CHANGES.md` dev section, preparing the release commit, and closing the tracking issue with a curated list of announcement highlights ([#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241), [#2353](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2353), [#2367](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2367))
- The **runtime-correctness review** effort closed out both tracking issues, with dozens of targeted, test-first fixes merged across the JS and Wasm runtimes covering marshalling, ephemerons/weak references, hashing, the Str engine, filesystem/Unix semantics, float parsing/formatting, and the Graphics backend ([#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263), [#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270), [#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280), [#2286](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2286), [#2287](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2287), [#2296](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2296), [#2313](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2313))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ran an independent multi-agent re-review of the Wasm runtime `.wat` files, surfacing new findings that were each turned into a fix, including WASI preopen/empty-path handling, format-string corner cases, and exception/marshalling edge cases ([#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263), [#2341](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2341), [#2343](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2343), [#2352](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2352))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) built the pure-Wasm **zstd/BLAKE2b** implementation and led a broader **Wasm runtime cleanup**, plus follow-up fixes for buffer-refill growth, bigstring array kinds, and `float32_hash` NaN/-0.0 normalisation ([#2249](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2249), [#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262), [#2329](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2329), [#2330](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2330), [#2331](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2331), [#2332](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2332))
- Test infrastructure work continued on [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)'s newly opened proposal to simplify version/backend/OS-conditional tests with `ppx_optcomp_light` and conditional expect tests, with an initial consolidation already merged ([#2360](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2360), [#2361](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2361), [#2358](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2358), [#2316](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2316))
- Documentation contributions from [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) landed cross-links between the Ocsigen family's docs (TyXML, ReactiveData, Lwt) and an updated hosted-package table, alongside [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)'s manual and odoc cross-reference cleanup ([#2322](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2322), [#2323](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2323), [#2351](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2351), [#2364](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2364), [#2362](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2362))
- CI hardening included cancelling superseded in-progress workflow runs and adding an OCaml 5.5 build job ([#2349](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2349))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review threads covered the ECMAScript baseline, a WASI linker crash, and Wasm evaluation-order and .wat-compatibility bugs.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) posted a curated set of **6.4.0 release-announcement highlights** on the release-tracking issue, pulling headline items (OCaml 5.5 support, WASI 0.1, dynlink/toplevel on Wasm, the Stack Switching effects backend) out of the cleaned-up changelog ([#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241))
- On [#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280) (float correctness fixes), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) and [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) discussed whether relying on `BigInt` was acceptable given it raises the minimum requirement from ECMAScript 6 to ES2020 — the exchange fed directly into the README's supported-engine baseline update ([#2347](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2347))
- Reviewing the **Wasm runtime cleanup** ([#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262)), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) asked whether the change could break external `.wat` runtime files; [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) confirmed none currently use conditionals, and the pair agreed to add a CI job checking that runtime files still convert to `wax`
- A **WASI linker crash** (`Invalid_argument("index out of bounds")`) surfaced in CI while linking the new `zstd-impl.wasm` module was diagnosed and fixed during review of the pure-Wasm zstd PR ([#2249](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2249))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) caught a Wasm bug where both operands of `i32.and` were evaluated unconditionally in the WASI preopen-prefix matching code ([#2341](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2341)); [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixed it in a follow-up PR ([#2352](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2352))
repositories.trends_summary: Systematic runtime-review fixes, JS/Wasm backend parity work, and test-infrastructure consolidation stood out this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **runtime-correctness review** methodology — systematic review of `.wat`/`runtime/js` sources producing many small, test-first PRs with native-verified expectations — continued to dominate the week's merges ([#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263), [#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270), [#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280))
- Continued **feature parity work** between the JS and Wasm backends, aligning hashing, float formatting, `nat` primitives, and zstd/BLAKE2b support across both runtimes ([#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263), [#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270), [#2339](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2339), [#2333](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2333), [#2249](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2249))
- **Test-infrastructure consolidation** is underway, moving version/backend/OS-conditional tests onto `ppx_optcomp_light` and conditional expect tests instead of ad-hoc `enabled_if`/`build_if` gating ([#2360](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2360), [#2361](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2361), [#2316](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2316))
- Post-release groundwork for **OxCaml 5.4** and further ocaml.org documentation packaging is already starting, immediately after the 6.4.0 release shipped ([#2342](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2342), [#2359](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2359))
repositories.contributors_summary: hhugo led runtime fixes and the 6.4.0 release, vouillon built the Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b runtime, balat and smorimoto contributed docs.

repositories.brief_summary: New `-open-cmi` flag and `box` type constructor landed alongside ongoing mode-polymorphism and ikind work across many open PRs
repositories.new_features_summary: New `-open-cmi` flag, a `box` type constructor, custom `or_null` payload shapes, `-O3` enabled, and several compiler error-message fixes
repositories.new_features:
- New `-open-cmi <file.cmi>` flag opens a module signature directly from a given `.cmi` file, taken verbatim without `-I`/`-H` lookup, and is processed before `-open` so a later `-open` can see modules it brings into scope, added by [Jakub Staroń](https://github.com/staronj) ([#6013](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6013))
- Introduced a `box` type constructor and its predefined type declaration in `typing/predef.ml`, giving a covariant boxed wrapper for values of arbitrary layout by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#5342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5342))
- `[@@or_null]` declarations now accept custom **zero-parameter, unused-parameter, and multi-parameter payload shapes**, inferring the result kind from the unary payload type and rejecting nullable payloads with a proper kind error, by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5830))
- `-O3` is now enabled, measured to speed up an internal build by 2-3%, by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#5778](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5778))
- Added **optimization fuel** infrastructure (`OPT_FUEL_RECORD`, `OPT_FUEL_THRESHOLD`, `OPT_FUEL_FAIL` environment variables) to bisect which optimization step causes a miscompilation, by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651))
- `read_library_info` now raises `Corrupted_unit_info` on read failure instead of an uncontrolled exception, giving a proper error on corrupted `.cmxa` files, by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6221](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6221))
- Fixed `ocamlnat -dparsetree`/`-dsource` printing the AST twice, by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6166](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6166))
- Suppressed the extra "gave up trying to find the simplest kind" diagnostic note on kind-mismatch errors when ikinds are enabled, since ikinds do not use fuel, by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#6197](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6197))
- Fixed an ikinds failure for recursive payload alias annotations by computing ikinds for temporary type declarations during `enter_type`, by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#6201](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6201))
- Replaced a `fatal_error` in the mode-hint solver with a printed warning for uses of the `alloc_as_value` morphism, so affected code now reports a hint instead of crashing, by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#6205](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6205))
- `caml_mem_name_map` stack-mapping calls (1-2us each) are now only made in the debug runtime, reducing `alloc_for_stack` overhead in release builds, by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#6280](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6280))
- `to_cmm` now optimises patterns such as `Nativeint.(logor (shift_left (of_int x) 1) 1n)` into a single bitwise-or instead of two shifts and an or, by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([#6103](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6103))
- The n-way join algorithm no longer joins equations on symbols, avoiding exponential slowdowns from meeting large mutually-recursive closure types, by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6284](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6284))
- Re-applied the fix for a bug in `meet` for array element kinds after a chain of reverts, by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#6233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6233))
- Merlin updated to track OCaml 5.4.0-ox1 (minus-39 to minus-40), by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250))
- Documented the interaction between **borrow regions** and **stack allocation**, explaining why a stack-allocated (`exclave_`) result currently cannot escape a borrow region, by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#6160](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6160))
- Fixed a broken link and strengthened a fragile link in the `jane/doc` manual, by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6154](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6154), [#6105](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6105))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs on mode polymorphism, ikinds, and the effects API remain open, while runtime4 CI support was dropped
repositories.activity:

repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion on the new Allocation mode axis and the effects API's yielding/local semantics drew detailed reviewer feedback

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion continued on a draft PR converting buffers to bytes, and a new issue requested system liburing support.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #153's buffer redesign drew review feedback while issue #156 raised building without vendored liburing.
repositories.activity:
- Draft PR converting the **buffer representation from bigarray/cstruct to bytes** by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) continued to receive review feedback this week, with discussion of buffer-size validation and safety trade-offs for reused buffers ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- New issue opened by [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) asking for a way to build against a **system liburing** instead of the vendored copy, prompting a response from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) on why the vendored version is pinned to a specific kernel ABI ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
repositories.discussions_summary: Reviewers debated buffer-safety trade-offs in the bytes-based buffer PR and the rationale for vendoring liburing.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On the **bytes-based buffer** draft PR, [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) raised concerns that reusing a buffer without zeroing it could leak private data unless exposed data is limited to what was actually filled, suggesting a `create_unsafe` variant and an `Iovec.to_cstruct` helper that shares the underlying memory without copying ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded that requiring caller-provided buffers of 2KB+ avoids the leak concern, since `uring` only needs to verify incoming buffer size rather than manage the memory itself ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explained the rationale for **vendoring liburing** — testing against a specific version tied to the Linux kernel ABI — in response to a request for system-library support, noting that Debian's unstable liburing currently matches the vendored version as a short-term workaround ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
repositories.contributors_summary: avsm advanced the buffer-type PR and answered a vendoring question; talex5 reviewed the PR; glondu opened a new issue.
repositories.contributors:
