- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the buffer-type PR, flagging a potential data-leak concern with buffer reuse and proposing API alternatives ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened an issue requesting the ability to build against system liburing instead of the vendored copy ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))

repositories.brief_summary: eio gained `chmod` support and fixes for FIFO reading and non-blocking opens, plus errno-based Process error propagation.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `Path.chmod` API, FIFO read fix, non-blocking confined-path opens, and structured `Eio.Process` errno errors.
repositories.new_features:
- Added `Path.chmod ~follow ~perm` for changing file mode bits, implemented for `Eio_linux`, `Eio_posix` and `Eio_windows` via new `Eio_unix.Private.chmod`/`chmod_unix` primitives, by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/785), closing [#764](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/764))
- Fixed `eio_posix` returning end-of-file immediately when opening a FIFO for reading with no writer connected, by adding a `Fifo_reader` that waits for readability before each read, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#857](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/857), fixing [#856](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/856))
- `eio_posix` now opens paths reached via `cwd` in non-blocking mode, matching the existing behaviour for paths opened via `fs`, which matters for FIFOs, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#855](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/855))
- `Eio.Process` failures from fork actions are now reported as `Unix.Unix_error` instead of an opaque `Failure`, with new `Argument_list_too_long`, `Permission_denied` and `Executable_format_error` error variants derived from the child's errno via a new `Fork_action.report_spawn_error`, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854), fixing [#556](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/556))
repositories.activity_summary: Draft PRs for `Eio_unix.Sockopt` and pseudoterminal support were rebased on this week's errno-propagation changes.
repositories.activity:
- Two draft PRs remain open and under active discussion: [Eio_unix.Sockopt](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575) for Linux socket options by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), and [pseudoterminal/`login_tty` support](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531) also by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which was rebased this week once its errno-propagation dependency landed ([#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854))
- **Errno propagation** for process spawning was merged, closing out a long-running discussion started by [Prophet](https://github.com/Innf107)'s original report and superseded PR ([#556](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/556), [#561](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/561), [#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854))
- **`chmod` support** landed for the POSIX-based backends, closing a long-standing tracking item under the broader missing-file-operations issue ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/785), [#764](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/764), [#510](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/510))
- **FIFO handling** on `eio_posix` was fixed in two stages, first tightening non-blocking opens and then adding a dedicated readable-wait for FIFO reads ([#855](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/855), [#857](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/857), [#856](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/856))
- The `eio_linux` fixed-buffers-by-default issue remains open, tracking a kernel bug on old ZFS versions triggered by `io_uring` fixed buffers, raised by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) following the earlier `io_uring` concerns discussion ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845), [#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on FIFO/`select` semantics and on `io_uring` safety concerns tied to a ZFS kernel bug.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) worked through the platform-specific quirks of FIFO blocking semantics on Linux vs macOS, noting that macOS `kevent` does not report readability changes when writers disconnect (referencing [golang/go#63937](https://github.com/golang/go/issues/63937)) and deciding to keep `eio_linux` behaviour unchanged while adapting `eio_posix` to match it ([#857](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/857), [#856](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/856))
- [Valentin Chaboche](https://github.com/vch9)'s earlier question about `io_uring` security concerns was closed this week, with [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) confirming that using a recent ZFS release avoids the underlying kernel issue, which also connects to the open discussion on disabling fixed buffers by default ([#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797), [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the errno-propagation PR and suggested consistent `pp_error` formatting that prints variant names, which [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) incorporated ([#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued hardening of POSIX file-descriptor edge cases and structured error handling around process spawning.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Ongoing work to close gaps against OCaml's `Unix` module surface, including `chmod`, socket options and pseudoterminals, tracked collectively under the missing-file-operations issue ([#510](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/510), [#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/785), [#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575), [#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- Repeated attention to subtle POSIX file-descriptor edge cases (FIFOs, non-blocking opens) across backends rather than a single fix, spanning two merged PRs in one week ([#855](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/855), [#857](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/857))
- Move toward structured, typed error variants (e.g. `Eio.Process.error`) instead of generic `Failure` exceptions for process spawning failures ([#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854), [#556](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/556))
repositories.contributors_summary: Five contributors were active this week through merged fixes, draft-PR rebases, issue triage and review discussion.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) merged the FIFO read fix and the non-blocking confined-path open fix ([#857](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/857), [#855](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/855)), opened the fixed-buffers-by-default issue ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845)), and reviewed both the `chmod` PR and the errno-propagation PR, contributing a documentation fix and formatting suggestions ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/785), [#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) authored and merged the fork-action errno propagation work ([#854](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/854)), and continued iterating on two draft PRs for socket options and pseudoterminal support, rebasing the latter on top of the newly merged errno changes ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575), [#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the `chmod` support PR for `Eio_linux` and `Eio_posix`, closing the tracking issue he had originally opened ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/785), [#764](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/764))
- [Valentin Chaboche](https://github.com/vch9) raised the original question about `io_uring` security considerations that was resolved this week ([#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) contributed a comment confirming that a recent ZFS version resolves the kernel-level `io_uring` issue discussed in [#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797)
- [Prophet](https://github.com/Innf107) originally reported the `Argument list too long` exception-handling issue and proposed an initial fix, later superseded by the errno-propagation PR ([#556](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/556), [#561](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/561))

repositories.brief_summary: Santiago Vila reported that the fib_par.exe test hangs on single-CPU systems, affecting Debian package builds (#135).
repositories.activity_summary: The week's only activity was a bug report about the test suite hanging when built on single-CPU machines.
repositories.activity: - [Santiago Vila](https://github.com/sanvila) opened [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/135), reporting that `fib_par.exe` hangs indefinitely when the test suite is run on a virtual machine with a single CPU, causing Debian package builds to be killed after 60 minutes of inactivity; the report notes the issue is reproducible by booting with `GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="nr_cpus=1"` and suggests skipping the parallel test when only one CPU is detected as a possible mitigation.
repositories.contributors_summary: Santiago Vila filed a detailed bug report on single-CPU build hangs; no code changes landed this week.
repositories.contributors: - [Santiago Vila](https://github.com/sanvila) filed [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/135), a detailed bug report describing how the `fib_par.exe` test hangs during Debian package builds on single-CPU virtual machines, including reproduction steps and a suggested mitigation of skipping the parallel test on single-core systems.

repositories.brief_summary: Patrick Ferris opened a PR reworking meio's logging, CLI stdout capture, and task-tree rendering ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76)).
repositories.activity_summary: PR #76 bundles a logging rewrite, a new --stdout CLI flag, and console rendering fixes, still awaiting merge.
repositories.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened **PR #76** ("Fixes"), a bundle of changes to the `meio` CLI, logging internals, and console rendering that has not yet merged ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- The `meio` binary gains a new `--stdout` option to redirect a monitored program's stdout to a file, and the `run` command now accepts an existing PID in addition to spawning a fresh executable and argument list ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- `Logging` is reworked with a `'a log` type and `info`, `warn`, and `debug` helpers that attach a monotonic timestamp tag to each message, and the reporter now also echoes formatted, timestamped log lines to stderr alongside the existing UI queue ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- `Meio.ui` now exposes the `Logging` module and takes a `start` timestamp argument, and `Task.t.logs` changes from a plain string list to a `(bool * string) list` so the console can distinguish user-emitted logs from internal ones ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- Console rendering fixes include truncating long task names to 32 characters (falling back to `<task>` when a name is missing), resizing the `INFO` column, and fixing a bug where task-tree column widths were computed from the wrong tuple element ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- The `meio.opam` file now pins a minimum OCaml version of 5.4.0, and the bundled examples (`burn.ml`, `burn_domains.ml`, `deadlock.ml`) were updated to match the CLI changes ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued incremental, solo-maintained refinement of meio's logging and terminal UI following on from prior CLI work.
repositories.emerging_trends: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) continues iterating on meio's observability features in a single-contributor cadence, following the earlier `Cmdliner`-based CLI rework merged in [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/75), with this week's [#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76) further extending logging and CLI ergonomics
repositories.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris authored and opened the week's only pull request, spanning CLI, logging, and console rendering changes.
repositories.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened **PR #76**, touching 17 files across the `meio` binary, the `Logging`, `Meio`, `Console`, `State`, and `Task` modules, and the bundled examples, adding a `--stdout` capture option, timestamp-tagged logging helpers, and console rendering fixes ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 and 4.14.4 compiler releases and their ocaml.org release-page documentation landed the same week.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 and 4.14.4 packages landed in opam-repository with matching ocaml.org release pages, plus Windows CI hardening.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Official **OCaml 5.5.0** compiler packages (`ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, `ocaml-variants`, `ocaml-system`, `ocaml-src`) added to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075)), with a follow-up fix restoring the relocatable compiler build template ([ocaml/opam-repository#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085))
- Matching **OCaml 5.5.0 release page** published on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org), covering module-dependent functions, polymorphic function parameters, GC improvements and around 60 new standard library functions, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- Official **OCaml 4.14.4 security bugfix release** packages added to opam-repository, containing two standard-library security fixes and an ARM64 compilation fix, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054))
- Matching **OCaml 4.14.4 release page** published on ocaml.org, documenting CVE-2026-34353 (`caml_ba_reshape` size-overflow) and CVE-2026-41083 (`Filename.quote_command` Windows argument quoting), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- `conf-pkg-config` fixed for **OpenBSD** to use the base-system `pkg-config`, by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082), closing [ocaml/opam-repository#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080))
- Windows CI reworked in opam-repository to **reduce depext-driven switch recompilations**, replacing a temporary `conf-pkg-config` workaround with a new `conf-allegro5.2` package, by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [ocaml/opam-repository#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058))
- New package **ktdeque**, a purely-functional double-ended queue extracted from a machine-checked Rocq proof, added by [Yann Régis Gianas](https://github.com/yurug) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30060](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30060))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- The week's central event was the **OCaml 5.5.0** feature release and **OCaml 4.14.4** security bugfix release, packaged in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075), [ocaml/opam-repository#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054)) and documented with new release pages on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) merged dozens of package releases, including upstream batch releases from ocurrent, savonet and landmarks, alongside continued Windows CI hardening ([ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069), [ocaml/opam-repository#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), [ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040))
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) activity centered on reviewing and merging the two compiler release pages, plus routine automated Planet blog aggregation and a documentation-gap report for `patricia-tree` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dozens of opam-repository releases and CI fixes merged while ocaml.org reviewed the two release pages and ran routine Planet scraping.
group_summaries.activity:
- Newly opened opam-repository PRs awaiting merge include **dune 3.24.0** by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), **par/par_cli** (a Programmable Agent Runtime for OCaml) by [JC](https://github.com/jcz2020) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)), and an **ocaml-r** 0.7.0 update for R 4.5/4.6 by [pveber](https://github.com/pveber) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30007))
- Bulk upstream batch releases merged: 10 packages from ocurrent by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069)), 8 from savonet/ocaml-ffmpeg and 4 from savonet/liquidsoap-release-assets by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), [ocaml/opam-repository#30057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30057)), and 3 from LexiFi/landmarks by [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30071](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30071))
- Individual package releases merged include **js_of_ocaml** 6.4.0 by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30088](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30088)), **tiny_httpd** 0.21 by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30074](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30074)), **goblint** 2.8.0 by [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30052](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30052)), and **docfd** 13.0.0 by [Darren Li](https://github.com/darrenldl) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30047](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30047))
- Closed without merge in opam-repository: the **stdcompat** multi-version update abandoned pending an upstream 5.5 compat branch, by [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30090](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30090)); and the name-reservation submissions **ioqwerty** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049)) and **aslp_lifter_ocaml** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30051](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30051))
- Review of the ocaml.org **5.5.0 release page** involved extensive wording and code-example corrections from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) before [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) merged it ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)); the **4.14.4 release page** was reviewed by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) before merging ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- Routine Planet feed scraping on ocaml.org continued to surface new community blog posts, including pieces from Ocsigen and robur.coop, while repeatedly flagging the same unreachable mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver and patricoferris feeds ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))
- [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) opened an ocaml.org issue noting the two most recent `patricia-tree` package releases have no documentation, possibly related to earlier documentation-generation issues ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3437), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The **OCaml 5.5.0** and **4.14.4** releases were coordinated across both repositories in the same week: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) authored the compiler packaging in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075), [ocaml/opam-repository#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054)) and the corresponding release pages on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) worked across both repositories: reviewing the ocaml.org 5.5.0 release page ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)) while releasing the **xdge** and **deepseek** packages and reviewing the **ioqwerty** name-reservation submission in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30070](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30070), [ocaml/opam-repository#30053](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30053), [ocaml/opam-repository#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049))
- On the ocaml.org 5.5.0 release PR, [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) raised a related, 8-month-stalled PR in the separate [ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals](https://github.com/ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals) repository about updating the online manual, tying the release documentation work to a third, adjacent repository ([ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals#12](https://github.com/ocaml-web/html-compiler-manuals/issues/12), discussed in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- The **dune 3.24.0** release thread in opam-repository surfaced test-suite regressions in cppo, camomile and lablgtk3 tied to a breaking change in `%{bin:...}` variable handling, with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) confirming the change should only affect invalid usage ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092))
- opam-repository maintainers reaffirmed the **utility-for-users policy** for package submissions, questioning the standalone **agentxm-example-tinyflags** package by [Craig Smitham](https://github.com/craigsmitham) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) and rejecting the **ioqwerty** name-reservation package by [Anton Matiash](https://github.com/amatiash) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049))
- On the ocaml.org **5.5.0 release PR**, [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) asked about the process for updating the online manual and observed that ocaml.org appeared to block merging while a change request was outstanding, which [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) said was not intended behavior ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- [Sebastien Marie](https://github.com/semarie) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) clarified that `pkg-config` has shipped in the **OpenBSD** base system since release 7.8, resolving the `conf-pkg-config` special-casing question in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080), fixed by [ocaml/opam-repository#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082))
- [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) raised missing documentation for the latest `patricia-tree` releases on ocaml.org, linking it to two previously reported documentation-generation issues ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3437), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem migration**: the official 5.5.0 compiler release and its ocaml.org documentation ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)), a relocatable-build fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085)), and compatibility updates in `baby` and `omod` all landed the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30084](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30084), [ocaml/opam-repository#30063](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30063))
- Continued **Windows CI hardening** in opam-repository reducing depext-driven switch recompilation and modernizing the workflow ([ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [ocaml/opam-repository#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058), [ocaml/opam-repository#30076](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30076))
- **AI/agent-tooling packages** entering opam-repository — a Programmable Agent Runtime SDK ([ocaml/opam-repository#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)) and an AgentXM example package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) — prompting maintainers to reaffirm the submission bar
- **Automated batch releases** from upstream CI bots continue to make up a large share of opam-repository merges (ocurrent [ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069), ocaml-ffmpeg [ocaml/opam-repository#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), liquidsoap [ocaml/opam-repository#30057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30057))
- On ocaml.org, automated `[scrape]` Planet PRs continued at a near-daily cadence, repeatedly reporting the same unresolved unreachable feeds ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))

weekly_summary.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 and 4.14.4 shipped alongside dune 3.24.0 and js_of_ocaml 6.4.0, with opam-repository and ocaml.org publishing matching packages and docs.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5.0, 4.14.4, dune 3.24.0 and js_of_ocaml 6.4.0 released, with opam-repository packages and ocaml.org release pages for both compilers.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Released **OCaml 5.5.0**, a feature release adding module-dependent functions, polymorphic functions as function arguments, a relocatable compiler, generalized local definitions, substring search/replace functions, external types, garbage collector improvements, and around 60 new standard library functions, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml release 5.5.0](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/5.5.0))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Released **OCaml 4.14.4**, a security bugfix release addressing CVE-2026-34353 (`caml_ba_reshape` size-overflow) and CVE-2026-41083 (`Filename.quote_command` Windows argument quoting), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml release 4.14.4](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/4.14.4))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Released **dune 3.24.0**, promoting directory targets from experimental to generally available, enabling the relocatable compiler by default for package management, and adding the `%{pkg:<package>:<section>:<path>}` pform for resolving package install files, by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/dune release 3.24.0](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/releases/tag/3.24.0))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Released **js_of_ocaml 6.4.0**, adding initial **OCaml 5.5.0** and **OxCaml** compiler support, WASI 0.1 support, a new variable-coalescing compiler pass, a pure-Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b runtime, and new `Promise` and `Fetch` library bindings, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml release 6.4.0](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/releases/tag/6.4.0))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Official **OCaml 5.5.0** compiler packages (`ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, `ocaml-variants`, `ocaml-system`, `ocaml-src`) added to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075)), with a follow-up fix restoring the relocatable compiler build template ([ocaml/opam-repository#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Matching **OCaml 5.5.0 release page** published on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org), by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Official **OCaml 4.14.4** packages added to opam-repository, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054)), with a matching ocaml.org release page documenting both CVEs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ `conf-pkg-config` fixed for **OpenBSD** to use the base-system `pkg-config`, by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082), closing [ocaml/opam-repository#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Windows CI reworked in opam-repository to reduce depext-driven switch recompilations, by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [ocaml/opam-repository#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New package **ktdeque**, a purely-functional double-ended queue extracted from a machine-checked Rocq proof, added by [Yann Régis Gianas](https://github.com/yurug) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30060](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30060))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The week's central event across the ecosystem was the release of **OCaml 5.5.0** ([ocaml/ocaml release 5.5.0](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/5.5.0)) and the **OCaml 4.14.4** security bugfix release ([ocaml/ocaml release 4.14.4](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/4.14.4)); no other core-repository activity was captured this week beyond the releases themselves.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.24.0** ([ocaml/dune release 3.24.0](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/releases/tag/3.24.0)) and **js_of_ocaml 6.4.0** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml release 6.4.0](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/releases/tag/6.4.0)) were both released this week, with js_of_ocaml adding day-one support for the new OCaml 5.5.0 compiler and for OxCaml; no other tools-repository activity was captured this week.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) packaged both new compiler releases the same week they shipped upstream ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075), [ocaml/opam-repository#30054](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30054)), and [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) published matching release-page documentation for each ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684)), alongside dozens of other package merges from ocurrent, savonet and landmarks, and continued Windows CI hardening ([ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069), [ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ No OxCaml-repository activity was captured this week, though [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) 6.4.0 added OxCaml compiler support as part of its own release.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ No multicore-repository activity was captured this week.

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Dozens of opam-repository package releases merged while ocaml.org reviewed release-page docs and ran routine Planet blog scraping.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Newly opened opam-repository PRs awaiting merge include **dune 3.24.0** packaging by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), **par/par_cli** (a Programmable Agent Runtime for OCaml) by [JC](https://github.com/jcz2020) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)), and an **ocaml-r** 0.7.0 update for R 4.5/4.6 by [pveber](https://github.com/pveber) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30007))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Bulk upstream batch releases merged: 10 packages from ocurrent by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069)), 8 from savonet/ocaml-ffmpeg and 4 from savonet/liquidsoap-release-assets by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), [ocaml/opam-repository#30057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30057)), and 3 from LexiFi/landmarks by [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30071](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30071))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Individual package releases merged include **js_of_ocaml** 6.4.0 by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30088](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30088)), **tiny_httpd** 0.21 by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30074](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30074)), **goblint** 2.8.0 by [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30052](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30052)), and **docfd** 13.0.0 by [Darren Li](https://github.com/darrenldl) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30047](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30047))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Closed without merge in opam-repository: the **stdcompat** multi-version update, abandoned pending an upstream 5.5 compat branch, by [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30090](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30090)); and the name-reservation submissions **ioqwerty** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049)) and **aslp_lifter_ocaml** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30051](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30051))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Review of the ocaml.org **5.5.0 release page** involved wording and code-example corrections from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) before [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) merged it ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)); the **4.14.4 release page** was reviewed by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) before merging ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3684))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Routine Planet feed scraping on ocaml.org continued to surface new community blog posts, including pieces from Ocsigen and robur.coop, while repeatedly flagging the same unreachable mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver and patricoferris feeds ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3686](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3686), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3687), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3689](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3689), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3690))

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **dune 3.24.0** release thread in opam-repository surfaced test-suite regressions in cppo, camomile and lablgtk3 tied to a breaking change in `%{bin:...}` variable handling, with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) confirming the change should only affect invalid usage ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ opam-repository maintainers reaffirmed the **utility-for-users policy** for package submissions, questioning the standalone **agentxm-example-tinyflags** package by [Craig Smitham](https://github.com/craigsmitham) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) and rejecting the **ioqwerty** name-reservation package by [Anton Matiash](https://github.com/amatiash) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30049](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30049))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ On the ocaml.org **5.5.0 release PR**, [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) asked about the process for updating the online manual and observed that ocaml.org appeared to block merging while a change request was outstanding, which [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) said was not intended behavior ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Sebastien Marie](https://github.com/semarie) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) clarified that `pkg-config` has shipped in the **OpenBSD** base system since release 7.8, resolving the `conf-pkg-config` special-casing question in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30080](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30080), fixed by [ocaml/opam-repository#30082](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30082))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) raised missing documentation for the latest `patricia-tree` releases on ocaml.org, linking it to two previously reported documentation-generation issues ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3685), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3437), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451))

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem migration**: the compiler release ([ocaml/ocaml release 5.5.0](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/5.5.0)), same-week opam-repository packaging and ocaml.org documentation ([ocaml/opam-repository#30075](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30075), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3688)), a relocatable-build fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#30085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30085)), day-one **js_of_ocaml 6.4.0** support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml release 6.4.0](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/releases/tag/6.4.0)), and compatibility updates in `baby` and `omod` all landed the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30084](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30084), [ocaml/opam-repository#30063](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30063))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml's Wasm backend** continued to mature, adding WASI 0.1 support, dynlink/toplevel support, and pure-Wasm zstd/BLAKE2b implementations that remove its remaining dependency on JavaScript-only shims ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml release 6.4.0](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/releases/tag/6.4.0))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Continued **Windows CI hardening** in opam-repository reducing depext-driven switch recompilation and modernizing the workflow ([ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30040), [ocaml/opam-repository#30058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30058), [ocaml/opam-repository#30076](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30076))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **AI/agent-tooling packages** entering opam-repository — a Programmable Agent Runtime SDK ([ocaml/opam-repository#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)) and an AgentXM example package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29905)) — prompting maintainers to reaffirm the submission bar
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Automated batch releases** from upstream CI bots continue to make up a large share of opam-repository merges (ocurrent [ocaml/opam-repository#30069](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30069), ocaml-ffmpeg [ocaml/opam-repository#30065](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30065), liquidsoap [ocaml/opam-repository#30057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30057))
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ __RUMINANT:multicore__ No activity was captured for the core, OxCaml or multicore groups beyond the OCaml compiler releases themselves this week, in contrast to the ongoing OxCaml/multicore development threads reported in [week 24](data/summaries/weekly/week-24-2026.json).
